_comment: The canonical corridor. Each property has a baseline value with a pool of equivalent sentences (wording drifts while meaning stays fixed), plus anomalous variants with their own pools. Remember MEANING, not wording.

meta.title: Hallway 8
meta.tagline: Locked in after hours. Follow the corridor to the fire exit.
meta.attempt: {n} times down this corridor, and this time it opened.
meta.attempt_first: First time through, and the corridor let you go.

steady: The fluorescent lights hum softly overhead.
The ceiling lights give off a steady, even glow.
Overhead, the fluorescents buzz at their usual pitch.
The lighting is flat and unremarkable, the way it always is.

flicker: One of the fluorescent tubes stutters, then holds.
The lights flicker, once, twice, before settling.
There's a faint strobe to the ceiling lights that wasn't there before.
dim: The corridor seems a shade darker than you remember.
The lights are dimmer here, as if half of them gave up.
A grey dimness has settled over everything.
warm: The light has a warm, yellowish cast to it.
The fluorescents glow oddly amber, almost like sunset.
The usual cold white has turned faintly golden.

vent: Somewhere, ventilation exhales a low, constant breath.
The air handling hums in the walls, steady as ever.
You hear the familiar drone of distant ventilation.

drip: A slow drip taps somewhere out of sight.
Water is dripping, you can't place where.
Tick. Tick. Something is dripping nearby.
silence: The ventilation has stopped. The silence presses on your ears.
It is completely quiet. Too quiet.
You notice the hum is gone. The stillness feels wrong.
footsteps: Footsteps echo somewhere behind you, then stop.
You hear footsteps that don't match your own.
For a moment there are two sets of footsteps.

travel: A faded travel poster clings to the wall, a beach, a blue sky.
The old poster of a sunny coastline is still taped to the wall.
On the wall, that washed-out advertisement for somewhere warm.
The travel poster hangs at its usual slight tilt.

gone: The wall is bare. A pale rectangle marks where a poster used to be.
The travel poster is gone; only the tape remains.
Nothing hangs on the wall now.
mountains: The poster shows snow-capped mountains, not a beach.
The coastline poster is now a photograph of grey mountains.
The advertisement pictures a cold summit under heavy cloud.
upside: The travel poster hangs upside down.
The beach on the poster is inverted, sky at the bottom.
Someone has flipped the poster; it hangs the wrong way up.

distant: Far down the corridor, a man in a grey suit walks away from you.
A suited figure moves ahead in the distance, back turned.
The man in grey is up ahead, as always, walking on.
Down the hall, a man in a suit recedes toward the vanishing point.

closer: The man in the grey suit is much closer than he should be.
The suited figure has stopped halfway, closer than before.
The man in grey is only a few paces ahead now, still turned away.
facing: The man in the grey suit has stopped. He is facing you.
Down the hall, the suited figure stands still, looking your way.
The man in grey is not walking. He is watching you.
gone: The corridor ahead is empty. No one is walking it.
There is no figure ahead this time.
The man in the grey suit is nowhere to be seen.
knows: The man in grey has turned and raised a hand, as if he knows you.
The suited figure faces you and beckons, slow and deliberate.
The man mouths something down the corridor. It might be 'again.'

exit: The illuminated {SIGN} sign glows at the far end.
Ahead, the {SIGN} sign hangs above the corridor.
The green {SIGN} sign points the way, as it should.
You can make out the {SIGN} sign in the distance.

reversed: The exit sign points the wrong way, EXIT ←.
The arrow on the exit sign now points back the way you came: ←.
dark: The exit sign is dark. No light behind the letters.
The exit sign has gone out.
Where the exit sign should glow, there is only a dead panel.
number: The sign reads EXIT 3. There was never a number on it.
A number has appeared on the sign: EXIT 3.
The exit sign now carries a numeral you don't remember: 3.

closed: A grey maintenance door sits shut in the left wall.
The service door on your left is closed, as usual.
There's the maintenance door, shut tight.

ajar: The maintenance door stands slightly ajar. Darkness inside.
The service door is open a crack, though you never saw it open.
The grey door is ajar; a thin black gap where it should be flush.
gone: There is no maintenance door in the left wall. Just smooth wall.
The service door is missing entirely.
The left wall is unbroken, no door at all.

away: A small CCTV camera in the corner points down the corridor.
The security camera watches the far end, angled away from you.
Up in the corner, the camera faces ahead, as always.

atyou: The CCTV camera has turned. It points directly at you.
The security camera is angled down at you now.
The camera in the corner faces you, its lens level with your eyes.
red: A red light blinks on the camera. It never used to blink.
The camera's recording light glows red for the first time.
The little LED on the camera pulses red.

dry: The grey floor tiles stretch away, dry and dull.
Underfoot, the usual scuffed grey tiles.
The tiled floor is dry, reflecting nothing.

wet: The tiles ahead shine with a wet, dark sheen.
The floor is wet, though you saw no one clean it.
A slick of water spreads across the tiles.
cracked: A crack runs across the floor tiles that wasn't there before.
One of the floor tiles is split down the middle.
The tiling is fractured in a jagged line ahead.

steady: The corridor feels exactly as long as it always has.
You carry the same low dread you've had since the lobby.
Everything feels ordinary, in the way this place is ever ordinary.

longer: The corridor feels longer this time, though you can't say why.
The far end seems further off than it should be.
The walls feel as if they've stretched since the last stretch.
forget: For a moment you can't remember how many doors you've passed.
You lose your count of the corridors, and it unsettles you.
You can't recall why you're down here at all.
calm: You feel suddenly, wrongly calm, as if you'd already escaped.
A strange relief washes over you that has no business being here.
You feel no fear at all, and that itself feels wrong.

repeat_inspect: You glance at the {THING} again. You aren't sure why.
Your eyes go straight to the {THING}, the way they always seem to now.
The {THING} draws your attention once more.
long_look: You spend longer looking than usual. You don't remember deciding to stop.
You realise you've been standing still. For how long?
Time slips. You were staring, and you don't know for how long.

cutscene: Something in the corridor changed, and you walked on past it.
If a stretch is not exactly as you remember, turn back. If it is, walk on.
intro: You stayed too late. The lobby doors are locked, the lights half-out.
A handwritten sign hangs over them: FIRE EXIT, USE CORRIDOR.
While a stretch of corridor is exactly as you remember it, walk on.
If anything has changed, the way has doubled back. Return the way you came.
The eighth fire door leads out.
Lose track of what you've seen, and the building returns you to the start.
win: The eighth fire door gives under your hands.
Cold air. A stairwell down to the street. The city, still there.
You were beginning to think the corridor had no end.

flashbacks: A desk lamp still burns a few doors back. A cup of coffee gone cold at its own ring. The clock reads a time the clock cannot read.
A phone is buzzing in a room you have not reached yet. The name on the screen is almost yours.
You had the lobby doors at six. You turned back for something you have already forgotten.
The man in grey held the fire door once, a long time ago, and looked at you the way you look at someone you are about to leave behind.
You have counted to eight before. You are almost sure of it. You are almost sure you never reached it.

exit.tempt: A door ahead stands open, a lit EXIT humming green above it. The street must be just through there.

lobby: You push through the lit door expecting the street.
It is the lobby you started in, the glass still locked, the night still black behind it.
The corridor you walked is simply gone. You are back at the beginning of a building that no longer has a way out.

reaction: The handle turns a little and stops, as if held from the other side.
You put your ear to it. Something on the far side goes quiet, the way a room does when you enter.
herring: The metal is warm, warmer than a locked door should be. It probably means nothing.
There is a draft under it that was not there before. Or was, and you never noticed.
fold: It opens onto a stretch of corridor you have already walked. The number in your head is gone. You are at the start again.

reaction: It swivels, unhurried, and settles on you. Nothing else moves.
The lens tilts down to your eyeline and holds there, patient.
herring: A red light you do not remember blinks once. It was probably always on.
For a second the lens fogs, as if breathed on from the inside.
fold: The camera follows your hand back the way you came, and your feet follow the camera, and you are at the start again.

reaction: Up close the beach is only dots of ink. A place you were going to go.
The corner has been peeled and pressed back so many times the paper has gone soft.
herring: The ink is still tacky. Freshly printed. It means nothing, and you know it means nothing, and still your hand pulls back.
The colours are too bright for how faded it looked a moment ago. A trick of the light.
fold: You lean in until the dots are all there is, and when you step back the corridor has reset around you.

figure.verb: call out to the man in grey
reaction: Your voice goes down the corridor and does not come back. He does not turn.
He pauses, a half step, the way you pause at your own name in a crowd. Then he walks on.
herring: For a moment his suit looks like yours. Grey is grey.
He is a little nearer than when you called. Just the walking, surely.
fold: He turns, finally, and it is a face you know too well, and the sight of it puts you back at the start.
